Private Mindo Birding

Custom Target Bird Tour Builder

Choose the birds you most want to see. We will review recent activity, routes, and feasibility to help plan a smarter private birding tour.

Choose Target Birds View Custom Private Tour
700target birds
7route areas
175iconic birds

Use your target bird list to prepare a smarter private route.

How it works

  • Select target birds.
  • Share dates, group size, and interests.
  • See a preview and request guide review.

No guarantee language. The report uses recent activity and local route knowledge.

Start with a list

Choose a starter list, then adjust individual birds before sending your request.

Skip to request form

Filter Birds

24 of 700 birds shown
0 selected

Browse by route area

Choose a route area to think like an itinerary: nearby gardens, higher cloud forest, lower Choco routes, or specialist private routes.

            WhatsApp

Select at least one target species before requesting review.

        

      

    

  







  

    Quick answer
    
What is included in the Target Bird Tour Builder?

    
The builder lets you choose target birds around Mindo, send your list to Mindo Bird Watching, and request a guide-reviewed opportunity report. We review your dates, route fit, recent activity signals, photography goals, group pace, and likely cloud forest conditions before suggesting a private route or tour plan.

    

      Best for: birders, photographers, families, and travelers with a focused wish list.
      Review includes: target species, route areas, timing, effort level, and guide notes.
      Local context: Mindo, Chocó Andino, feeders, trails, dawn routes, and private access options.
      Next step: submit your list, then confirm dates and route expectations with a guide.
    

  






  

    

      
Best for / not best for

      
Use these cards to decide whether to build a target list or start with a standard private tour.

    

  

  

    

      
Best for target birders

      
Choose this tool if you have species you hope to prioritize, such as toucans, tanagers, hummingbirds, Cock-of-the-Rock, antpittas, or Chocó specialties.

      
Start choosing birds

    

    

      
Best for photographers

      
Tell us if clean light, feeders, perches, hides, or slower field time matters more than covering many kilometers in one day.

      
Photography planning

    

    

      
Best for flexible private tours

      
Use the report to shape a half-day, full-day, or multi-day route with realistic expectations based on season, weather, and active sites.

      
View tours

    

    

      
Not best for guarantees

      
Birds move. The review improves your route choices, but it does not guarantee any species. Ethical birding and habitat protection come first.

      
Recent sightings

    

  






  

    

      
Target bird tour builder FAQs

      
Short answers for planning a custom birding route around Mindo.

    

  

  

    
What birds can I expect?
You can select from hundreds of Mindo and Chocó Andino birds. The final route depends on your target mix, recent activity, time available, weather, access, and whether you prioritize photography or a broader species list.

    
Do I need a guide?
A guide is strongly recommended for target birds because many species require route timing, vocalization knowledge, ethical distance, and active site awareness. The builder helps MBW understand what matters most to you before recommending a route.

    
Can I use it for one day?
Yes. The builder is useful for a Quito to Mindo day trip, a Mindo-based morning, or a multi-day plan. Shorter trips need tighter priorities, while longer trips can combine more habitats and target groups.

      Ready for guide review?
      
Turn your target list into a realistic Mindo birding route.

      
Send your selected birds, travel dates, group size, birding level, and photography goals. MBW will review your list and help shape a private route around real Mindo conditions.

      

        Request guide review
        Ask on WhatsApp
      

    

    

      
What happens next

      
    
        
  1. You choose target birds and send your request.
    2. 
        
  2. We review route fit, timing, access, and recent activity signals.
    3. 
        
  3. We suggest a practical private route or tour plan.
    4. 
        
  4. You confirm dates, pickup logistics, and guide availability.
    5. 
      

    

  






  

    
Useful Preview
The preview helps guests understand route focus and difficulty without promising guaranteed sightings.

    
Recent Activity Review
After the list is sent, we check recent eBird activity and local knowledge before recommending the final route.

    
Responsible Route Areas
We show general route areas and keep sensitive locations for internal guide review.
